channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The ‘internet of beings’ is the next fronteir that could change humanity and healthcare
item.description: In the 1966 film Fantastic Voyage, a spacecraft and its crew are shrunk to microscopic size and injected into the body of an injured astronaut to remove a life-threatening blood clot from his brain.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: The DJI Osmo Action 6 can now record 8K video
item.description: A new update is rolling out now for the DJI Osmo Action 6 that brings with it a host of improvements, including 8K.
item.dc:creator: David Nield, TechRadar

item.title: The 34 best shows of 2025: TechRadar's top TV picks on Netflix, Prime Video, HBO Max, and more
item.description: 2025 has been jam-packed with top-tier TV shows – here are TechRadar's favorite ones of the last 12 months.
item.dc:creator: Tom Power, TechRadar

item.title: DoorDash launches Zesty, an AI app for finding local food
item.description: DoorDash launches Zesty, an AI-powered social app that recommends restaurants through conversational search, now testing in San Francisco and New York.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Samsung Galaxy S26 is tipped to get satellite and gaming boosts
item.description: We're getting a better idea of what to expect from the Samsung Galaxy S26, but the price is very much to be confirmed.
item.dc:creator: David Nield, TechRadar

item.title: Fujifilm's epic 40TB tape cartridge is here - despite what Elon Musk says, the future of offline data storage looks very bright
item.description: Fujifilm’s 40TB LTO-10 tape reinforces offline storage relevance as enterprises balance ransomware risk, regulatory demands, and growing AI driven archival volumes.
item.dc:creator: Efosa Udinmwen, TechRadar

item.title: Top 10 Nvidia stories of 2025 – From data center to AI factory
item.description: 2025 was not just about faster GPUs. It was about the fundamental re-architecture of the enterprise data center from a storage/retrieval hub to a manufacturing plant for intelligence – or the AI factory.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: ServiceNow $12 billion deal spree is ‘deja vu’ of CEO’s SAP days
item.description: After years of eschewing big mergers, ServiceNow Inc. is on a deal spree. It has spent at least $12 billion this year on acquisitions or strategic investments.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: The Switch 2 has had a brilliant first year, but cracks in performance are already starting to show
item.description: Nintendo’s year was dominated by Switch 2 speculation and the arrival of the system, but there’s plenty more that happened, too.
item.dc:creator: Lloyd Coombes, TechRadar

item.title: Best Boxing Day sales 2025: deals from Amazon, Currys, John Lewis, and other leading retailers today
item.description: Looking to do some post-Christmas shopping? We've laid out all of today's best Boxing Day sales in one easy to read list right here - including tech, appliances, home, and much more.
item.dc:creator: Alex Whitelock, TechRadar

item.title: The hidden cost of launching AI initiatives without strategic clarity
item.description: In the 1980s and 1990s, many large manufacturing companies pursued an offshoring strategy — not always because a careful analysis showed a clear link between offshoring and achieving their business objectives, but because their competitors… The post The hidden cost of launching AI initiatives withou
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: How to watch Midnight Mass 2025
item.description: The BBC bring live coverage of the First Mass of the Nativity. Here's how to watch Midnight Mass 2025 online and from anywhere.
item.dc:creator: Tom Wardley, TechRadar

item.title: Microsoft is not rewriting Windows in Rust
item.description: A job posting by a Microsoft engineer sparked excitement about a project “to eliminate every line of C and C++ from Microsoft by 2030”, replacing it with Rust — but alas for fans of the memory-safe programming language, it turns out this is a personal goal, not a corporate one, and Rust isn’t necess
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 3D-printed housing project for student apartments takes shape
item.description: Denmark's 3D-printed student village proves automation builds 36 apartments faster than traditional methods. Skovsporet project shows housing future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Get started with Python’s new native JIT
item.description: JITing, or “just-in-time” compilation, can make relatively slow interpreted languages much faster. Until recently, JITting was available for Python only in the form of specialized third-party libraries, like Numba, or alternate versions of the Python interpreter, like PyPy.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Deno adds tool to run NPM and JSR binaries
item.description: Deno 2.6, the latest version of the TypeScript, JavaScript, and WebAssembly runtime, adds a tool, called dx, to run binaries from NPM and JSR (JavaScript Registry) packages. The update to the Node.js rival was announced December 10; installation instructions can be found at docs.deno.com.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: FCC bans new foreign-made drones over national security concerns
item.description: The Federal Communications Commission on Monday said it would ban new foreign-made drones, a move that will keep new Chinese-made drones such as those from DJI and Autel out of the U.S. market.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Trump administration moves to overhaul how H-1B visas are granted, ending lottery system
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security is replacing its lottery system for H-1B visas with a new approach prioritizing skilled, higher-paid foreign workers.
item.dc:creator: Joey Cappelletti, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Interoperability and standardization: Cornerstones of coalition readiness
item.description: The DoD’s emphasis on modular, open architectures promotes innovation within shared boundaries, keeping future systems adaptable, affordable and compatible. The post Interoperability and standardization: Cornerstones of coalition readiness first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Android Sound Notifications help you catch key alerts
item.description: Discover Android's new Sound Notifications feature that alerts you to smoke alarms, doorbells, and baby cries even when wearing headphones.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Stop letting ‘urgent’ derail delivery. Manage interruptions proactively
item.description: As engineers and managers, we all have been interrupted by those unplanned, time-sensitive requests (or tasks) that arrive outside normal planning cadences. An “urgent” Slack, a last-minute requirement or an exec ask is enough to nuke your standard agile rituals.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: OPM to hire critic of HR consolidation plan
item.description: Don Bauer, the former CTO at the State Department’s Bureau of Global Talent Management, is returning to government to lead the HR modernization effort. The post OPM to hire critic of HR consolidation plan first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Waymos blocked roads and caused chaos during San Francisco power outage
item.description: Waymos stopped in the middle of intersections during a mass power outage in San Francisco over the weekend, causing the robotaxi company to suspend service for a day.
item.dc:creator: Jaimie Ding And Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 8 old programming languages developers won’t quit
item.description: The computer revolution has always been driven by the new and the next. The hype-mongers have trained us to assume that the latest iteration of ideas will be the next great leap forward. Some, though, are quietly stepping off the hype train.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Starlink in the crosshairs: How Russia could attack Elon Musk's conquering of space
item.description: NATO-nation intelligence services believe Russia is developing a weapon to target Starlink satellites.
item.dc:creator: John Leicester, Associated Press, KPRC 2
